card.title: mazer: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Coordinate mobile safe-area, shared overlay geometry, and browser layout through one no-overlap contract and proof matrix.
card.objective: Make menu, Options, play, and Pause remain readable and operable across phone, browser, PWA, and restored-window surfaces without per-screenshot offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
No completion claim relies on desktop-only proof for a phone or restored-window defect.
Physical-device findings remain open until operator screenshots confirm the deployed behavior.
discoveries: The operator correctly rejected c7b1540e because logical Phaser bounds were contained while a second text-texture scale still painted glyphs across rails.
The 2x game canvas must be the sole backing-resolution owner; text textures now stay at resolution 1.
nextActions: Collect operator iPhone screenshots from exact preview head 4984d16c.
Keep physical-device and native-browser proof open until the operator confirms the corrected rendering.
evidence: Focused render verification passed 58 tests plus typecheck; full verification passed 363 tests and the production build.
Exact remote deployment dpl_CV7pHtLRhdH5e626cgGb4eCDTrGx passed manual 393x852 menu, Options, play, Pause, scroll, and zero-console-error inspection.

entry.headline: Physical glyph double scaling removed from PR 76
completed: Removed Phaser text as a second backing-resolution transform owner.
Added a capture gate that rejects any surface with textTextureResolution other than 1.
Manually inspected the exact remote 393x852 menu, play, Options, Pause, and scroll endpoints.
discovered: The prior verifier measured display-object geometry instead of the larger painted text texture, allowing a visible failure to pass.
next: Collect operator physical iPhone screenshots before production promotion or completion.

card.title: mazer: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive browser geometry and preserve only semantic settings so viewport changes cannot replay stale layout.
card.objective: Recompute all menu, Options, play, and Pause geometry from the active content rectangle while preserving explicit player preferences only.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout-derived geometry is never persisted as player preference state.
A recalculated layout cannot overlap, escape the viewport, or hide essential controls.
Normal reload preserves supported semantic settings with safe migration defaults.
Narrow, wide, phone, and restore endpoints have deterministic evidence.
discoveries: Device-scale canvas rendering plus independent Phaser text resolution created two physical transforms despite correct logical geometry.
Render-path ownership must be asserted in the capture report, not inferred from bounds.
nextActions: Complete physical iPhone review of preview head 4984d16c.
Retain native browser chrome and maximize or restore as separate remaining evidence.
evidence: Local 393x852 device-scale-2 capture and exact remote preview inspection both show contained painted text across all touched surface families.

entry.headline: Browser proof now asserts one text transform owner
completed: Set text textures to the resolution-1 baseline under the existing 2x game canvas.
Exposed textTransformOwner and textTextureResolution in UI diagnostics.
Rejected the visibly broken resolution-2 experiment and retained only the passing correction.
discovered: The former bounds-only gate could not distinguish logical containment from physical glyph overflow.
next: Use physical screenshots to decide whether this exact preview can advance.

card.title: mazer: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one responsive menu and play status component for time, Rank or AI Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze level from the correct context.
card.objective: Give menu and active play the same compact status language while keeping score authority traceable to the canonical scorer.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same status-panel formatting and layout contract.
Panel text remains centered, contained, and readable at phone and desktop widths.
Panel width, gap, and play Pause placement satisfy shared viewport diagnostics.
Displayed score is traceable to canonical run-quality data rather than a presentation-only calculation.
discoveries: The play status rail failure came from painted glyph scaling after layout, not from the shared panel's measured rectangle.
The corrected status rows now use the same single-transform text path as every overlay.
nextActions: Review corrected menu and play status rows on the operator's physical iPhone.
Identify and consume the finalized canonical 0 to 100 scorer field before completion.
evidence: Exact remote 393x852 menu and play status screenshots show rail clearance after removing the second text transform.
Canonical score authority remains explicitly non-terminal.

entry.headline: Shared status glyphs now match measured panel geometry
completed: Removed post-layout text texture enlargement from the shared status renderer.
Re-ran local and exact deployed phone-scale status proof.
Kept the card in Review pending physical operator confirmation and scorer authority.
discovered: Correct logical padding was being invalidated by the independent texture-resolution multiplier.
next: Confirm the corrected status presentation with operator iPhone screenshots.
